What is the BET Award?

A'ja Wilson is making headlines again. She has added another milestone to her remarkable 2026 campaign by winning the Sportswoman of the Year award at the BET Awards. The Las Vegas Aces superstar received the honor after another dominant stretch in the WNBA.The recognition comes during a standout season for Wilson, who is fresh off a commanding performance in Las Vegas' 107-99 victory over the Chicago Sky. Despite briefly leaving the game after rolling her ankle, the four-time WNBA MVP returned to finish with 30 points and 15 rebounds.Wilson topped a star-studded list of nominees to capture the Sportswoman of the Year award. The category featured elite athletes from several sports, reflecting one of the deepest fields at this year's BET Awards.The nominees included basketball stars Angel Reese and Flau'jae Johnson, boxing champion Claressa Shields, tennis standouts Coco Gauff and Naomi Osaka, Olympic track athletes Gabby Thomas and Sha'Carri Richardson, and Olympic gymnast Jordan Chiles.Wilson's latest accolade arrives during another exceptional WNBA season. She powered the Aces past Chicago with 30 points, 15 rebounds, four steals and three blocked shots while helping Las Vegas improve to 14-5.Aces coach Becky Hammon praised her franchise centerpiece after the victory."She just has her finger print all over the game. When she wants basketballs, she just goes and gets them. That's a luxury to have."The victory marked Las Vegas' 10th win in its past 12 games and further strengthened its position among the WNBA's top contenders.Wilson gave the Aces a brief scare in the fourth quarter against Chicago after appearing to roll her ankle with 7:20 remaining.She left for the locker room as concern spread throughout the United Center. The absence proved short-lived. Wilson returned just moments later with 7:05 left on the clock and immediately resumed her impact, scoring 10 more points while recording a crucial steal with just over three minutes remaining to help seal the victory.Her quick return eased fears of a significant injury and allowed Las Vegas to close out another important road win.Wilson continues to lead the league on both ends of the floor, producing MVP-caliber numbers through 18 regular-season games.Her combination of scoring efficiency, rebounding and rim protection has once again placed her among the favorites for another MVP award.Established in 2001 by Black Entertainment Television (BET), the BET Awards celebrate outstanding achievements by African Americans and other minorities across music, film, television, sports and philanthropy.The ceremony is traditionally held at the Peacock Theater in Los Angeles and is broadcast live on BET and other Paramount-owned networks. The award trophy, designed by sculptor Carlos "Mare139" Rodriguez, draws inspiration from the words "aspire, ascend, achieve."
Article Summary: A'ja Wilson wins BET Award 2026 in the Sportswoman of the Year category. The Las Vegas Aces center received the honor after another dominant stretch in the WNBA. Wilson is fresh off a commanding performance in Las Vegas' 107-99 victory over the Chicago Sky.

Article Title: One last wicket: Ben Stokes bids farewell to Test Cricket in emotional Trent Bridge finale
Article Text:
Ben Stokes announces his farewell

Poll Vote & Share your view What will you remember most about Ben Stokes' Test career? Match-winning performances under pressure His leadership and fighting spirit 3k+ users shared opinion today 5k+ users already voted today 3k+ users shared opinion today Share Opinion

Brief scores:

As Ben retirement announcement was being made official at Trent Bridge on a blazing Sunday afternoon, the crowd stood up and started cheering. The England captain, at the top of his bowling mark, stopped for a moment, showed no reaction and charged in. And what resulted seemed surreal.A tiring Stokes induced a nick off New Zealand batter Zak Foulkes’ bat and the second slip Harry Brook completed a catch, the team celebrating as if there’s no tomorrow. The cheer across the ground turned into an eruption of emotion — a celebration of England’s greatest allrounder after Ian Botham and the best world cricket has seen since Jacques Kallis. Stokes was coming back from a one-match ban following a bar brawl in this Test match. On the morning of Day 4, he had told his teammates that this would be his last Test, saying “reasons can wait for another day”. Playing only one format, one felt Stokes was good enough for a few more years of Test cricket. But it wasn’t to be.Probably, this latest controversy was the last straw in a career that has had its share of troubles — he was once banned in 2017 after a bar brawl in Bristol. Injuries, too, had taken a severe toll on his body, Stokes was often forced to take breaks in the past few years.An all-format matchwinner, an ODI and T20 World Cup winner, scorer of one of the greatest Ashes centuries of all time, Stokes had achieved everything. But numbers can’t explain Stokes — the 35year-old was way more than that. He was an iconoclast, a player who could face fire with fire.His 135 against Australia at Headingley, taking his team to a miraculous one-wicket win will always be remembered as one of the greatest Test innings ever played. Playing with No. 11, the attack that he launched on Australia was a turning point for English cricket.It wasn’t one-off. Every time England were in trouble, Stokes seemed to rise, be it during the ODI World Cup final win in Lord’s in 2019 or the 2022 T20 World Cup final triumph against Pakistan in Melbourne. Stokes was everywhere, guiding England through choppy waters.New Zealand 438 & 288/9 dec (D Mitchell 100*, R Ravindra 94, J Archer 4/53) vs England 354 & 103
Article Summary: Ben Stokes announced his retirement from Test cricket on Sunday. The 35-year-old was coming back from a one-match ban following a bar brawl in this Test match. On the morning of Day 4, he had told his teammates that this would be his last Test, saying ‘reasons can wait for another day’

Article Title: George Kittle exposes Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's surprise wedding rule that even close friends must obey
Article Text:
George Kittle Plans to Break Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's "No Gifts" Wedding Rule (Gregg DeGuire/Variety via Getty; Bruce Glikas/WireImage)

What did George Kittle say about Taylor Swift at Tight End University?

What are Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding plans?

San Francisco 49ers tight end George Kittle is not exactly planning to follow the rules at Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce 's wedding. Speaking to ExtraTV at the Tight Ends and Friends Concert in Nashville on Tuesday, the 32-year-old confirmed his invitation to the upcoming ceremony and casually admitted he is already thinking about bending one particular instruction. Swift and Kelce reportedly told guests no gifts, which Kittle found amusing but not entirely binding. "They said absolutely no gifts. But I was thinking, Travis, for some reason, really likes old coins, so I might get him an old coin," the San Francisco 49ers tight end said, joking, "Sounds expensive, too."George Kittle's appearance at Tight End University in Nashville gave him more than just a chance to mentor the league's best pass-catchers. It gave him a front-row seat to just how much Swift brings to the people around her, even with her wedding days away.Taylor Swift made a surprise appearance at the Pinnacle on Tuesday night and joined Lainey Wilson for a duet that drew the loudest reaction of the evening. Before the duet, Swift joked it was a "special request by a very special tight end" and pointed directly at Kittle. His mother was apparently there, and Swift made time to speak with her too."She's just hanging out, talking to my mom. Shout out to Taylor for talking to my mom again. But she's just so kind, and it's awesome to have her around," Kittle said.Tight End University, co-founded in 2021 by Travis Kelce, Kittle, and Greg Olsen, is a three-day summit held annually in Nashville. It combines film sessions, on-field work, and recovery programming, with the concert serving as its biggest public-facing moment and directing proceeds toward causes championed by all three founders.Kittle made clear that Swift's presence carried extra weight this year. "She's just such an awesome person to give us her time this close to their wedding," he said. "So we're just so thankful that they're here."Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce announced their engagement in August 2025, and the summer wedding speculation has been building steadily since. Reports have linked the couple to a July ceremony at Madison Square Garden, though neither side has officially confirmed a date or venue.In the days leading up to TEU, both spent time with their closest circles. Swift was spotted at her Watch Hill mansion in Rhode Island with longtime friend Abigail Anderson and other close friends for what appeared to be a girls' weekend. Kelce, meanwhile, was seen around California with what looked like a bachelor party group that included his brother Jason, Patrick Mahomes, comedian Druski, and Swift's own brother Austin.As for Kittle, he is also managing his own timeline. He is recovering from an Achilles tear suffered during the Wild Card loss to the Philadelphia Eagles in January 2026 and targeting a Week 1 return against the Los Angeles Rams.
Article Summary: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce reportedly told guests no gifts at their wedding. San Francisco 49ers tight end George Kittle plans to break that rule. Kittle was speaking at Tight End University in Nashville on Tuesday. Swift made a surprise appearance at the concert and sang with Lainey Wilson.

Article Title: Two auto-drivers, one destination: The making of India's new long jump queen Ancy Sojan
Article Text:
Ancy Sojan (ANI Photo)

Ancy Sojan (TOI Photo)

KOCHI/BHUBANESWAR: When Ancy Sojan overhauled a 22-year-old national record in the women’s long jump on Saturday, it shook awake Nattika, a tiny fishing hamlet in Kerala’s Thrissur district.Some 2,000 km away from the sand pit in Bhubaneswar where she had landed with 6.88m, two local auto-rickshaw drivers vicariously fulfilling their own sporting dreams as Kerala legend Anju Bobby George ’s long-standing record disappeared into the sand. Anju’s 6.83m was set at the 2004 Athens Olympics.One was Ancy’s father, Sojan ET. The other, her childhood coach Sanoj VV — “Kannan Mash” to all in Nattika.Neither men made it beyond the district level as athletes, stuck as they were in the traffic snarl that is the lack of opportunities in Indian sport. On Saturday, they were finding redemption through Ancy’s majestic 6.88m leap.“People laughed at me when I predicted in 2019 that Ancy would break Anju Bobby George’s record,” Ancy’s earliest coach told TOI, “She was barely 18 then. See, today my prediction has come true.”Father Sojan, currently recovering from a minor mishap suffered last week or else he would have been by his daughter's side, said, “I am living my dream through my daughter.I could not progress because we didn’t have the facilities to train. But here she is now...”Ancy hails from Nattika’s lower-income Syrian Christian community. Money was scarce, leading Sanoj to ferry passengers in his auto-rickshaw. “We are not financially well off,” remembered Sanoj, “Our income depends on how much I earn from driving my auto-rickshaw, and it varies from day to day. Right now, I am recovering from an injury, so it has affected our earnings.”“This record is dedicated to my father,” Ancy said in Bhubaneswar, “He would often say that we didn’t have any record at the national level in the family. For him, this is a reward for all the trust he placed in me and all the sacrifices he made over the years. This is my way of giving something back to him and making him proud.”The Sojans comprise Jansy, 47, Ancy’s homemaker-mother. Younger son Dominic, 24, is studying business administration while the youngest, Anjali, 20, aims to follow in her sister’s footsteps as a long jumper.Not far, Sanoj, like Sojan, was a district-level gold medallist in high jump, sporting ambitions ended before they began. “I am not a trained coach myself. I began my academy for budding athletes like Ancy, so I could pass on my experience to them,” Sanoj revealed.Sanoj first realised Andy’s potential when she was just a teen. “At the U-14 State School Games in 2015, Ancy jumped to 4.93-m to win bronze. It was remarkable considering she was so tiny back then, just 13 years,” he recalled.In 2019, Ancy burst onto the national athletics consciousness, registering a 6.26-m jump at the National School Athletics Meet in Punjab’s Sangrur.Following Ancy’s record-breaking leap in Bhubaneswar on Saturday, Sanoj noticed what some experts in long jump may have missed. “Till a year back, Ancy’s take-off extension had an issue. Not on Saturday. It was flawless,” he elaborated.Sojan reckoned that his daughter is yet to reach her full potential. “She is not 100% there yet,” he said, “She needs to improve her jump and holding time. But I’m proud of her for erasing a 22-year-old national record set by Anju. She can now breach the 7-m mark,” he added.Sanoj agreed. “I believe she can cross the 7-m barrier,” he said.Anju, the OG, knows it will happen. “I am proud of her…watched her jump with a smile on my face. Long jump has always been a Malayalee speciality. Proud that a Keralite broke my record,” Anju said before leaving Bengaluru to meet the new star.Young Ancy too believes that the 7-m is within reach.“Anju ma’am told me, ‘This is nothing. You’ve broken the record, but now you have to keep doing it consistently and win medals at the World Championships and Olympics. This is a world-class jump, and soon we are moving towards 7-m,’” a breathless Ancy said.The 25-year-old, for long the country’s third-best long jumper behind Anju proteges Shaili Singh and Lakshadweep’s Mubassina Mohammed, will be chasing the 7-m. It has been on her mind for some time, but that demands a few technical adjustments.“I had some issues with my landing. My legs were reaching the right position, but my body wasn’t following properly. After that, I worked on my landing technique using the hanging bar and other drills, and that helped me reach 6.75m,” she said.Progression has to be gradual. And smart.“If I suddenly try too much for 7m, it could affect my jump. Right now, we are focusing on raising my consistency,” said the Asian Games silver medallist, “The 6.70m range should become my minimum level. It will happen. I’m much more confident about that now.”Ancy’s record jump is testament to the fact that while sporting talent can emerge from anywhere, it is usually from the modest backgrounds of India. And as in Ancy’s case, it can even be nurtured by two autorickshaw-plying men who once harboured similar dreams.
Article Summary: Ancy Sojan breaks 22-year-old national record with 6.88m jump in Bhubaneswar. Anju Bobby George’s 6.83m was set at the 2004 Athens Olympics. Ancy hails from Nattika, a tiny fishing hamlet in Kerala's Thrissur district.

Article Title: Look back: Jerry Jones built a $13 billion Cowboys empire, but these 7 moments still follow him
Article Text:
Dallas Cowboys owner Jerry Jones has led the franchise since 1989, winning three Super Bowls while also facing decades of controversy and criticism. (Image via Getty)

Jerry Jones threatened to sit Dallas Cowboys players over anthem protests

Jerry Jones’ fight with Roger Goodell got ugly after Ezekiel Elliott’s suspension

The Dallas Cowboys cheerleader settlement put the franchise under another spotlight

The Alexandra Davis legal fight became deeply personal and very public

Firing Tom Landry was the first shock of the Jerry Jones era

The Jimmy Johnson split still feels like the Cowboys’ original sin

Randy Moss , Joey Galloway and Roy Williams became expensive reminders

Jerry Jones has owned the Dallas Cowboys since 1989. The business case is not hard to see. Forbes valued the Cowboys at $13 billion in 2025, keeping Dallas as the NFL ’s most valuable team for the 19th straight year.The football case is harder to defend. The Cowboys have not won a Super Bowl since the 1995 season. Jones has built an empire, but the bill has come in public fights, legal messes, locker-room questions and roster decisions that still annoy Dallas fans.Jones drew national criticism in 2017 when he said Cowboys players would not play if he believed they disrespected the flag during the national anthem.“If there’s anything that is disrespectful to the flag, then we will not play,” Jones said after a loss to the Green Bay Packers, according to the Cowboys’ official site. “Understand? If we are disrespecting the flag, then we won’t play. Period.”The comments came during a leaguewide debate over players kneeling to protest police brutality and racial injustice. Jones framed it around the flag. Critics saw it as an owner drawing a hard line against player protest.In 2017, NFL Commissioner Roger Goodell suspended Dallas Cowboys running back Ezekiel Elliott for six games after the league investigated domestic violence allegations.Jones backed Elliott and challenged Goodell’s handling of the case. ESPN later reported that Jones threatened Goodell while pushing back against the commissioner’s power.“I’m gonna come after you with everything I have,” Jones told Goodell, according to ESPN. “If you think Bob Kraft came after you hard, Bob Kraft is a pu**y compared to what I’m going to do.”The NFL later sought more than $2 million from Jones for legal fees tied to the Goodell contract dispute and Elliott-related litigation, according to ESPN.In 2022, ESPN reported that the Cowboys paid a $2.4 million confidential settlement after four cheerleaders accused longtime team executive Richard Dalrymple of voyeurism in 2015.The allegations included claims that Dalrymple used his security access to enter the cheerleaders’ locker room while they were changing. ESPN also reported a separate allegation involving inappropriate photos of Charlotte Jones, Jerry Jones’ daughter and a Cowboys executive.Dalrymple denied wrongdoing. The Cowboys said an internal investigation found no evidence of wrongdoing. The settlement still raised obvious questions about workplace safety, power and accountability inside one of the NFL’s most visible franchises.Jones also faced a public legal battle with Alexandra Davis, who said he was her biological father.Davis filed a lawsuit in 2022 seeking recognition that she was not legally bound by an agreement reached when she was a child. ESPN reported that the case involved allegations that Jones had paid Davis’ mother and set up trusts while not admitting paternity.In July 2024, ESPN reported that a countersuit trial involving Jones, Davis and her mother ended abruptly after Jones agreed to drop his countersuit if the women dismissed pending lawsuits against him.It was not a football story. It was still a Jerry Jones story, because his private life once again became public business.Jones did not ease into ownership. After buying the Cowboys in 1989, he fired legendary coach Tom Landry, the only head coach the franchise had ever known.Landry had gone 3-13 in 1988, but the handling mattered. ESPN noted that Jones later admitted he made a mistake in how he handled the firing and said he should have given Landry at least one more season.Jones wanted a new era. He got one. He also made his first major Cowboys controversy almost immediately.The Cowboys won back-to-back Super Bowls under Jimmy Johnson after the 1992 and 1993 seasons. Then the partnership broke.Jones wanted control over everything, from marketing to roster decisions. Johnson wanted credit and authority. ESPN reported that Jones’ comment that any one of 500 coaches could win with the Cowboys bothered Johnson.Johnson left. Barry Switzer won another Super Bowl with the team Johnson helped build. Dallas has not reached a Super Bowl since.That is why the Johnson split still matters. It was not just drama. It was the fork in the road.Jones’ roster swings also left marks. Dallas passed on Randy Moss in the 1998 NFL Draft and selected defensive end Greg Ellis at No. 8. Ellis became a solid player. Moss became one of the greatest wide receivers in NFL history. ESPN noted that Moss never lost to the Cowboys in seven career games.Jones also traded two first-round picks to the Seattle Seahawks for Joey Galloway in 2000. Galloway tore his ACL in his first regular-season game with Dallas.In 2008, Jones traded three picks, including a first-round pick, to the Detroit Lions for wide receiver Roy Williams. He then signed Williams to a five-year, $45 million deal. Williams never became the No. 1 answer Dallas paid for.Jones made the Cowboys richer than anyone. He also made sure they were never boring. For better and worse, that has been the Jerry Jones deal.
Article Summary: Jerry Jones has owned the Dallas Cowboys since 1989. The Cowboys have not won a Super Bowl since the 1995 season. Jones has built an empire, but the bill has come in public fights, legal messes, locker-room questions and roster decisions that still annoy Dallas fans.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Tim Cook: "Life is fragile. We're not guaranteed a tomorrow so…" - a reminder to live fully now
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Apple CEO Tim Cook (Image source: Wikipedia)

Quote of the day by Tim Cook

"Life is fragile. We're not guaranteed a tomorrow so give it everything you've got."

Who is Tim Cook

The wake-up call behind the quote by Tim Cook



What is the meaning of the quote by Tim Cook

Why this Tim Cook’s quote is relevant

How to apply this quote by Tim Cook in daily life

Other famous quotes by Tim Cook

"Let your joy be in your journey, not in some distant goal."

"For the most important decisions in your life, trust your intuition, and then work with everything you have to prove it right."

"The sidelines are not where you want to live your life. The world needs you in the arena."

"We want to leave the world better than we found it."

Most of us live as though time is unlimited. We put off the call, the trip, the apology, the dream, telling ourselves there's always next year. Tim Cook, the head of Apple , offers a sharper way to look at it. Life is fragile, he said. We're not guaranteed a tomorrow so give it everything you've got. It's a plain, almost blunt reminder, and that's exactly what gives it force. None of us truly knows how much time we have. We act as if tomorrow is promised, yet it never really is. Cook isn't trying to frighten anyone. He's trying to wake us up. If today might be all you can count on, then the only sensible response is to stop holding back, stop waiting for the perfect moment, and pour yourself fully into the life you have right now.Tim Cook is the chief executive of Apple, one of the largest and most valuable companies in the world. He took over in 2011 from Steve Jobs, the company's legendary co-founder, at a moment when many doubted anyone could fill those shoes.Born in Alabama in 1960, Cook joined Apple in 1998 and ran its operations for years before stepping into the top job. Under his leadership, the company grew into a giant worth trillions.He's known less for showmanship than for being calm, disciplined and deeply principled, the steady hand rather than the dazzling visionary. Which makes his reflections on life itself, rather than on technology, all the more worth hearing.Cook has returned to this idea more than once, including in speeches to graduating students just setting out in the world. The thought didn't come from nowhere. He has spoken about a frightening health scare earlier in his life, a moment that forced him to confront how quickly everything you take for granted can change.That kind of experience tends to rearrange your priorities in a hurry. When you've felt how thin the line really is, the small worries shrink and the big questions grow louder. Are you spending your days on what actually matters? Are you holding back out of fear? Cook came away convinced that life is too fragile, and far too short, to coast through on autopilot. The quote is the distilled version of that lesson, handed to the rest of us before we have to learn it the hard way ourselves.The quote has two halves that work together. The first is a hard fact. Life is fragile, and tomorrow isn't guaranteed to anyone, no matter how young, healthy or careful they happen to be. The second half is what we're meant to do about it. Give it everything you've got.Put together, the message is a call to live without holding back. If our time is genuinely uncertain, then half-hearted living becomes a kind of waste. Why save your best effort, your honesty, your love, for some later day that may never actually arrive? Cook is arguing for full commitment to the present, not because life is grim, but precisely because it's so precious. The fragility isn't a reason to despair. It's a reason to show up completely while you can.It's easy to drift through life on autopilot, quietly assuming there's always more time to start really living. We delay joy, postpone risks, and keep our fullest selves in reserve for later. This quote cuts straight through that habit.It doesn't ask for anything dramatic. It simply asks you to stop treating tomorrow as a certainty and to bring more of yourself to today. In a culture that constantly nudges us to wait, to play it safe, to defer happiness until some future milestone is reached, Cook's reminder lands hard. The time to give your life everything you've got isn't someday. It's now, because now is the only part any of us is actually promised.You can take this to heart without overhauling your whole life overnight.Stop postponing what matters. The call you keep meaning to make, the thing you keep meaning to say, the plan you keep delaying, pick one and do it this week.Give your full effort. Whatever today holds, your work, your relationships, your time with the people you love, put your whole self into it rather than half.Take the worthwhile risk. If fear is the only thing standing between you and something meaningful, treat that as a reason to move, not a reason to wait.Appreciate the ordinary day. You don't need a crisis to value your time. A little gratitude for a normal, healthy day is its own quiet way of giving life everything you've got.It's a striking message from a man who runs one of the busiest companies on earth. With everything competing for his attention, Cook's advice comes down to something simple and human. Don't wait. Don't hold back. The one day you can be sure of is this one, so give it all you have. The rest of life, fragile as it is, tends to reward the people who do.
Article Summary: Tim Cook is the chief executive of Apple, one of the largest and most valuable companies in the world. He took over in 2011 from Steve Jobs, the company's legendary co-founder, at a moment when many doubted anyone could fill those shoes. Cook's quote is a call to live without holding back.

Article Title: “Three-against-one situation”: El Rubius opens up on being “targeted” in MrBeast’s viral $1M challenge
Article Text:
El Rubius reacted honestly after his elimination from MrBeast’s $1 million challenge, calling it “unfair” and claiming he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings. His response felt real and balanced, especially after MrBeast’s team later sent him a cake, softening the moment.

El Rubius reflects on a tough and “unfair” exit







El Rubius did not just walk away from MrBeast’s $1 million streamer challenge. He opened up. Soon after his elimination, the Spanish creator shared a raw reaction with fans, giving them a closer look at what the experience felt like from inside the game. It was not polished or carefully framed. It felt real, and that is exactly why it caught attention so quickly.The clip and his response have since fueled conversations across the streaming space. Some viewers believe intense competitions like this naturally lead to uneven situations. Others feel Rubius may have had a point about being singled out. Either way, his words have added a human layer to a challenge that many only saw as entertainment.Speaking through an Instagram Story, Rubius did not hide his frustration, but he also made sure to thank his supporters. He said, "Guys, I just got knocked out of the game. It was a bit of an unfair elimination, but hey, at least I managed to walk away with $50,000 for you all, which I’ve already distributed. It went to the mods, the subs, and the non-subs alike."He further added, "I’m happy, of course, though admittedly, it was incredibly tough.I wasn't really a fan of the challenge itself, either. But then again... it was basically a three-against-one situation the entire time. I mean, they were targeting me specifically the whole way through. I kept trying to go after RaKai, but, well, he actually just got eliminated in the end. So, at least justice was served for Abraham. Abraham, if you’re watching this, we were all rooting for you, and standing right there with you."His message carried mixed emotions. There was disappointment, yes, but also pride in how he handled things. Giving away his $50,000 winnings to his community stood out as a gesture that meant more than the result itself.He continued with another honest note, saying, "And in the end, karma finally caught up with that little punk. Sorry, I’m being a bit toxic right now, but let's just hope the MrBeast team doesn't see this! Guys, thank you all so much. Seriously, thank you for all the support and for just being there."He added, "Every time I asked for something in the chat or whatever it was, you guys were right there. You’re incredible. I’m a little disappointed in myself, personally, mostly for not performing better, but hey, I gave it my all, and we still walked away with $50,000, so we’ve got to look on the bright side. All right, guys, big hugs to all my brothers out there!"Interestingly, the story did not end with frustration. Later that day, Rubius revealed that MrBeast’s team had sent him a cake after his exit. It was a small gesture, but it shifted the mood. It showed that even in a high-pressure challenge, there is still room for respect.In the end, Rubius’ reaction felt honest and grounded. That is what stayed with people.
Article Summary: El Rubius was eliminated from MrBeast's $1 million streamer challenge. He called it "unfair" and said he was targeted in a three-against-one situation. Despite his frustration, he thanked fans and gave away his $50,000 winnings.

Article Title: Ipl Ticket Scam: IPL craze costs techie Rs 1.46 lakh in fake RCB vs CSK ticket scam
Article Text:
Image credit: AI generated pic

Watch Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy Devdutt Padikkal press conference: Chinnaswamy pitch, Tim David’s power & RCB’s batting strategy

RCB register their second win

Poll Do you think the demand for IPL tickets is too high every season? Yes, it's excessive. No, it's just right.

NEW DELHI: IPL comes and the demand for tickets and passes go soaring up by the fans. The desperation to get inside the stadium to watch their favourite teams in action in the IPL go high and high. When the tickets get sold out, the demand gets too hig, and the cricket fans even ask for tickets in black. But for a 25-year-old techie the craze for IPL tickets for RCB vs CSK turned too costly and he was duped for a massive Rs. 1.46 lakh.The techie, a resident of B Narayanapura, fell into a trap by a post posted by a fraudster who posed himself as a ticket seller on Instagram.As per a report in Deccan Herald, the techie alleged in a complaint to Mahadevapura Police that an individual claiming to be Sumit Biswal promised he could arrange IPL tickets along with food coupons.As per the techie, Biswal was introduced him as a senior supervisor for ticket counter at M Chinnaswamy Stadium"He asked me to come near the stadium gate number 10, saying someone would deliver the tickets within minutes, and even sent an email confirmation to gain my trust," the complainant told Deccan Herald."Initially, I agreed to buy two tickets for Rs 3,700 each,but he kept asking for more money under various pretexts such as refundable security deposits, additional ID cards and food coupons.Trusting him, I made multiple payments - even using my mother’s bank account after exhausting my own limits—and ended up transferring around Rs 1.46 lakh," he added.Bhuvneshwar Kumar’s incisive spell of 3-41, coming after a marauding batting show from Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal, propelled Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) to a commanding 43-run victory over Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday.The contest saw RCB’s batting unit dismantle CSK’s attack with relentless hitting, as David’s 70 not out off just 25 balls and Patidar’s unbeaten 48 off only 19 balls provided for the late fireworks after Padikkal’s fluent 50 off 29 balls had set the tone for the daunting total.Their combined effort lifted RCB to a daunting 250/3, thus setting a new record for highest total in IPL 2026. In reply, CSK faltered under scoreboard pressure, with Sarfaraz Khan’s 50 off 24 balls and Prashant Veer’s 43 the lone acts of resistance amid a string of failures from the top order.Bhuvneshwar was at his disciplined best and even crossed the 200-mark in terms of wickets in IPL, with others also chipping in as CSK were bowled out for 207 in 19.4 overs. The comprehensive win also meant RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL’s history.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB) beat Chennai Super Kings (CSK) in their IPL 2026 encounter at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium on Sunday. Tim David, Rajat Patidar and Devdutt Padikkal put on a marauding batting show to lift RCB to a daunting 250/3. RCB have registered four consecutive triumphs over CSK for the first time in the IPL's history.

Article Title: Clavicular Viral Controversy: "Trans women are.....": Clavicular’s viral moment with trans women sparks fresh conversation on internet culture
Article Text:
A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Instead of conflict, the exchange highlights shared influence and mutual awareness. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration rather than ownership.

Clavicular and trans women share a surprising realization about online trends







The video of Clavicular, a short-video producer, is going around the Internet, yet not the reason why. There is no yelling or over-the-top drama. It is a mere, everyday talk that seems almost novel nowadays, in the age of internet, which is overflowing with content. The scene is easy-going, the tone of the narrative is straightforward, and the characters appear more concerned with conversation than combat.The message that is being addressed is the flow of the conversation. It discusses internet culture, the way people communicate with one another and the place of trends. Rather than bringing out a debate, the encounter towards curiosity. It quietly brings attention to something that a lot of people who use the internet notice but don't often say out loud. People in different communities often have a bigger impact on each other than they think.The conversation begins with a confident and lighthearted comment from one of the trans women present. She says, "You know that trans women are like the OG lip masters, right? We are very talented in the program. Like, I do the peptides, I'm on Zeta." Her tone carries a mix of pride and humor, setting a relaxed vibe at the table.Clavicular responds in a way that keeps the mood steady but adds a different angle.He says, "That is funny because you guys were using a lot of the words in like the South Korean subreddits in like 2014." It’s not confrontational. If anything, it feels like an observation. He points to how certain words and ideas may have traveled across different online spaces over time.Another voice joins in and brings the moment full circle. "Guys, we're like the OGs. I saw a couple of posts about that. We're like copying each other." That line hits in a simple but honest way. It reflects something familiar. Online culture is rarely created in isolation. It grows through sharing, borrowing, and sometimes even unknowingly repeating.That's what makes the clip so powerful. It doesn't want to make a point or win an argument. It only shows a group of people talking, listening, and figuring something out together.This moment feels different in a space where viral content often leads to fights. It reminds people that conversations can be calm, thoughtful, and still important. And sometimes, that honest silence is what makes it work.
Article Summary: A viral clip featuring Clavicular and trans women is gaining attention for its calm and honest tone. The conversation explores how online language and trends move across communities. Viewers are engaging with its thoughtful nature, as it reflects how internet culture evolves through collaboration.

Article Title: Whatever I do, it is for this city that I love, says Brinda Miller
Article Text:
Brinda Miller, recipient of the Bombay Times Art and Culture Visionary Award, sees the honour as a testament to her lifelong dedication to Mumbai. Inspired by her father, Nana Chudasama, a former Sheriff who championed city beautification, Miller has been a driving force behind the Kala Ghoda Arts Festival's growth.





Receiving the Bombay Times Art and Culture Visionary Award was more than just another accolade for artist and cultural curator Brinda Miller. For her, the honour reaffirmed a lifelong commitment to Mumbai. a city she says has shaped her identity and inspired her work for decades.Speaking about what drives her, Brinda says, "Being a fine artist and a creative person, I had always wanted to do something for Mumbai, the city that I love. I had to give back and that is what my jounney is all about."Her connection with the city, she says, began at home. "I was raised in a family that truly loves Mumbai. My father, Nana Chudasama, was Sheriff of Mumbai and he always strove to beautify the neighbourhoods of Bombay. I watched him distribute saplings to encourage people to grow more trees. Many cultural events were organised to draw attention to the beautification of the city. It is all in my genes."One of Miller's most enduring contributions has been her role in shaping the Kala Ghoda Arts Festival into one of India's most celebrated cultural events. Looking back on that journey, she says, "Twenty-five years ago, I volunteered to help organise the Kala Ghoda Arts Festival, and today I am proud to be instrumental in bringing it to its present glory."She is quick to acknowledge the collective effort behind the festival's success. "I am grateful to The Times of India Group for always supporting Mumbai's only such arts, heritage and cultural event. Most of the headliners who won their awards alongside me have contributed to the success of the Kala Ghoda Arts Festival. I have merely been a catalyst in making it possible."The award ceremony also coincided with an emotional personal milestone. "June 17 was also my father Nana Chudasama's birth anniversary. If he is watching me from above, I think I made him proud with this award."For Miller, every initiative, whether in art, public spaces or culture, has always been about giving back to the city she calls home. As she sums it up, "Like him, whatever I do, I do it for this city that I love. Call it Bombay or Mumbai, it is the appreciative people that make it the success that it is."
Article Summary: Brinda Miller is the recipient of the Bombay Times Art and Culture Visionary Award. Inspired by her father, Nana Chudasama, a former Sheriff who championed city beautification, Miller has been a driving force behind the Kala Ghoda Arts Festival's growth.

Article Title: Jalna police bust honey-trap gang, woman kingpin among six arrested
Article Text:
The six accused in police custody

Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar

:

TOI





Jalna police have arrested six people, including an alleged woman kingpin with a history of honey-trap offences, for abducting a man, assaulting him and robbing him of cash and mobile phone.The complainant was also forced to transfer money through UPI after the accused recorded an obscene video. The accused also tried to extort more money from the relatives and friends of the complainant.According to the police, the woman kingpin, Sapna Santosh Nikalje (23), is a habitual offender with multiple honey-trap cases registered against her in Jalna and Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar districts.Police alleged that her modus operandi involved luring victims, recording obscene photographs or videos, threatening to make them public, taking them to isolated places or lodges, summoning accomplices, assaulting the victims and robbing them. In an earlier case registered at Karmad police station, the victim allegedly died by suicide fearing social stigma.Following the detection, Jalna superintendent of police (SP) Teghbir Singh Sandhu appealed to the citizens who may have fallen prey to similar crimes to approach the police without fear.The latest incident took place on the night of June 20 under the jurisdiction of Chandanzira police station. According to police, the victim was intercepted on New Mondha Road and taken towards the Dry Port area.After the victim lodged a complaint on June 23, a case was registered at Chandanzira police station. Taking serious note of the offence, Sandhu and additional superintendent of police Vikram Sali directed police inspector Balasaheb Pawar to crack the case at the earliest.Sali, while talking to, said, “The coordinated efforts of the investigation team led to the arrest of all the accused within four hours of identifying the suspects and the recovery of crucial evidence.”The remaining five accused have been identified as Avinash Ganesh Zhote (26), Arjun Chaman Bahadur Rajput (34), Shankar Manik Jadhav (26), Atish Satish Gaikwad (19) and Sachin Laxman Kanthale (32).
Article Summary: Jalna police have arrested six people, including a woman kingpin, for alleged honey-trap offences. Police alleged that her modus operandi involved luring victims, recording obscene photographs or videos, taking them to isolated places or lodges, summoning accomplices and robbing them.

Article Title: Rain disappears, farmers now worried
Article Text:
Due to the reduced rainfall, sowing for the kharif season was affected

Kolhapur





: After two days of heavy rain in Satara and Kolhapur districts, very little rain was recorded in the last 24 hours ending on Saturday 5 pm.In Satara district, Koyna dam catchment received 3 mm of rain, Mahabaleshwar received 5 mm, and Navaja received 7 mm, resulting in less inflow into the Koyna dam.After a dry spell that stayed for nearly 20 days, heavy rain was received on June 23 and 24, in Satara, Patan, Jawali, Kandati, Mahabaleshwar, and Navaja region. Water also started flowing into the Koyna dam. However, the intensity of rain slowed down again since Thursday.On Saturday, the live water storage of Koyna dam reduced to 8.90 TMC (8.88%) with an inflow of merely 231 cusecs. In the dam catchment areas of Kolhapur district, 0 mm of rainfall was recorded in the last 24 hours.Due to the reduced rainfall, sowing for the kharif season was affected. Meanwhile, the ban on water lifting for agricultural purposes, which was temporarily lifted in Sangli and Kolhapur districts by the Irrigation Department owing to farmers protests, was imposed again on Saturday.Vishwas Salokhe, a farmer from Panhala, said, “Sowing has been delayed. Due to the current cloudy weather, there has not been sufficient rain required for the growth of crops. If there is good rain in the next few days, it will be of great benefit to rice, soybean, groundnut, maize, and other kharif crops. It will also help in increasing the water storage in the dams”.As per the Indian Meteorological Department (IMD), Kolhapur, Sangli, and Satara districts possessed a Yellow alert for Sunday with the probability of thunderstorm accompanied by light rainfall and gusty winds up to 30-40 kmph.
Article Summary: After two days of heavy rain in Satara and Kolhapur districts, very little rain was recorded in the last 24 hours ending on Saturday 5 pm. In Satara district, Koyna dam catchment received 3 mm of rain, Mahabaleshwar received 5 mm, and Navaja received 7 mm. Due to the reduced rainfall, sowing for the kharif season was affected.

Article Title: Sangli MLA Sudhir Gadgil suffers minor injury in accident
Article Text:
The SUV carrying MLA Gadgil that met with an accident

Kolhapur

:





An SUV carrying BJP MLA Sudhir Gadgil met with an accident on the Nagpur-Ratnagiri National Highway near Sangli on Saturday evening after a two-wheeler suddenly took a U-turn in front of the vehicle, the police said.The two-wheeler rider, whose identity is yet to be ascertained, was injured and taken to Miraj Civil Hospital in a 108 ambulance for treatment. Gadgil too suffered a minor injury to his finger, and was taken to a private hospital in Sangli, treated, and later discharged.The accident occurred between Vaddi and Krishna Ghat Road while Gadgil was travelling towards Ankali. The police said the collision took place when a two-wheeler abruptly cut across the MLA’s vehicle, leaving little time for the driver to react.Gadgil had earlier travelled to Ugar in Karnataka for an event and was returning to Sangli when the incident took place. The impact triggered the deployment of airbags in the SUV, preventing serious injuries.Gadgil was accompanied by his personal assistant Nagesh Bhokare, bodyguard Amol Kanase, photographer Vikas Gundap, and driver Ganpati Darekar. All four escaped unhurt.Traffic movement on the busy highway was briefly disrupted before National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) officials and Sangli police reached the spot and restored normalcy.
Article Summary: SUV carrying BJP MLA Sudhir Gadgil met with an accident on the Nagpur-Ratnagiri National Highway near Sangli. The two-wheeler rider, whose identity is yet to be ascertained, was injured and taken to Miraj Civil Hospital in a 108 ambulance for treatment. Gadgil too suffered a minor injury to his finger, and was taken to a private hospital in Sangli, treated, and later discharged.

Article Title: Teen and sibling thrashed after spat on train in Navi Mumbai
Article Text:
Another incident of verbal spat between a 16-year-old teenage boy and a 20-year-old youth took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night, after the youth accidentally stamped on the teen's foot. (File photo used for representational purpose)





NAVI MUMBAI: A day after the Borivli train murder incident following a violent altercation between two commuters that took place on June 23, yet another incident of verbal spat between a 16-year-old teenage boy and a 20-year-old youth took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night, after the youth accidentally stamped on the teen's foot.The altercation later spilled on to the street, when the youth accompanied by his two friends waylaid the teen near Seawoods Grand Central Mall around 9. 30pm and assaulted him with kicks and fist blows.As the teen's elder brother intervened to rescue him, the trio assaulted him as well in which he sustained a hand fracture.The injured brothers were identified as Rony Rana Shaikh (22) and Alkas Rana Shaikh (16).While, the accused youth was identified as Rohit Surendra Bhosale (22), all residing in Tata Nagar slum pocket, sector 10, CBD-Belapur near Konkan Bhawan.Accused Rohit Bhosale and his two unidentified aides were booked under the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) sections for voluntarily causing grievous hurt and joint criminal liability.
Article Summary: A day after the Borivli train murder incident following a violent altercation between two commuters that took place on June 23, yet another incident of verbal spat took place in a harbour line train in Navi Mumbai on June 24 night. The altercation later spilled on to the street, when the youth accompanied by his two friends waylaid the teen and assaulted him with kicks and fist blows.

Article Title: 4 youngsters trapped in elevator of Navi Mumbai high-rise for 90 minutes; rescued safely by firemen
Article Text: NAVI MUMBAI: Four youngsters were trapped inside an elevator between the third and fourth floors of Tulip CHS in Sector 34B, Kharghar, for about 90 minutes from 3 am on Friday due to a power outage in the residential area.The Kharghar fire brigade received the emergency call at 4.25 am and reached the society at 4.35 am.Firefighters rescued the trapped individuals — two men and two women, all aged between 20 and 22 — by forcing open the elevator door from outside using a crowbar and a battery-operated spreader tool.Prashant Darekar, a leading fireman at Kharghar, said,"It took us less than 5 minutes to rescue the trapped youngsters. While, one woman was a tenant in the building, the other three were her friends who had come to drop her home after they returned from a night out."
Article Summary: Four youngsters were trapped inside an elevator between the third and fourth floors of Tulip CHS in Sector 34B, Kharghar, for about 90 minutes from 3 am on Friday due to a power outage. Firefighters rescued the trapped individuals — two men and two women, all aged between 20 and 22 — by forcing open the elevator door from outside.

Article Title: SIR is about building a cleaner electoral roll: State CEO Chockalingam
Article Text:
State chief electoral officer S Chockalingam

Q. The

Special Intensive Revision

will start tomorrow. What should voters expect?

Chief Electoral Officer

S Chockalingam

(SC) I

Q There is widespread concern that genuine voters could be deleted

SC I

Q How important is field verification in urban areas?

SC

Q What should voters do if they cannot locate their previous electoral record?

SC I

Q What are the three parts of the form?

SCI

Q What documents will voters need to produce?

SCI

Q What if an elector’s name is mapped, but is in an anomaly list?

SC I

Q Can a voter submit documents to the BLO instead of waiting for a notice?

SC I

Q Some critics say this exercise is about deleting voters

SC I





The field verification is the most important phase of Special Intensive Revision (SIR) because the tentative mapping we carried out so far will be physically confirmed. The guiding principle of the exercise is that no eligible voter should be left out. We have completed mapping around 76% of voters using the previous electoral rolls. It is tentative because similar names may have been wrongly mapped and only a physical verification by the booth-level officer (BLO) and by the elector can confirm whether the mapping is correct. If it is, the elector will provide basic details and sign the form, which confirms the mapping. Until then, our mapping is not final. If it is wrong or unmapped, the elector can give correct details for the mapping and sign.The exercise will ensure the opposite. The law empowers the Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) to determine eligibility after the prescribed legal process. Every voter can establish their eligibility before a decision. If a person’s name is available in the electoral rolls of the previous SIR, that itself is an important reference. Even if the person’s name cannot be traced, those of the parents in previous SIR electoral rolls are important links.We have carried out mapping using historical electoral records. But, this mapping is tentative and is valid only after physical verification and the elector’s signature.Urban areas are a much bigger challenge. People have shifted houses, changed constituencies and sometimes moved to different districts or states over the last two decades. Therefore, while the overall mapping is around 76%, in urban areas it is below 60%. BLOs can ask voters where they were enrolled in the previous SIR. Even if they do not remember the polling station, many remember the assembly constituency or the MLA they voted for. The Election Commission’s online search facility available on the ECI website (‘Search Your Name in the Last SIR’ button in https://voters.eci.gov.in) enables citizens to locate previous electoral details across the country. The part number or polling details help locate the earlier record. Our public advertisements request citizens, particularly urban voters, to keep these details ready before the BLO visits.There are several alternatives. If they cannot trace their previous electoral record or establish linkage through their parents’ names in earlier SIRs’ rolls, they can rely on any of the prescribed documents. During the house-to-house visit, the elector must provide basic information and sign the form. Supporting documents can be submitted during the claims and objections period after notices are issued.Every elector whose name appears in the electoral roll as on June 18, 2026 will receive a form pre-filled with the voter’s details. If the photograph needs updating, the BLO will take a new one using a cellphone. This is not necessary unless the existing image is blurred. The form has three parts. The first part is pre-filled with the voter’s existing details, including name, EPIC number, address, polling details and photograph, which the elector verifies during the BLO’s visit. The second part seeks details linking the elector or their parents to the last Special Intensive Revision (2002-2004) electoral roll, including the assembly constituency, part and serial number, or the name of a parent or qualifying relative appearing in the earlier roll. The third part contains the elector’s declaration, signature or thumb impression, cellphone number, or that of a close family member and Aadhaar number. If the voter cannot be mapped during the verification, they will still receive the form but will later be issued a notice to submit supporting documents during the claims and objections period before the Electoral Registration Officer.The SIR instructions list 11 documents (Aadhaar is 12th document but only as an identity proof), but this is only an indicative list and not an exhaustive one. Equivalent documents can also be accepted. For example, if someone does not possess a matriculation certificate, a transfer certificate or another equivalent educational document can be produced. ERO will verify these documents with the govt departments wherever necessary. All 11 documents need not be submitted, only one is required.Around 20% of the mapping has anomalies like the surname does not match, too many people mapping with a single name in previous SIR roll, etc. In these cases, relevant documents need to be submitted. Suppose a person’s name has changed after marriage, then a marriage certificate may be sufficient. If there is a discrepancy in age, then a birth certificate or school leaving certificate would be adequate. The purpose is only to resolve the particular issue identified as an anomaly. The Electoral Registration Officer should be satisfied with the certificate submitted.At present, the prescribed process is that notices will first be issued and documents must be submitted thereafter during the claims and objections period.No. People should understand the difference between a summary revision and an intensive revision. In the former, the existing electoral roll remains the base document and additions or deletions are made. In the latter, a new electoral roll is prepared. Naturally, this removes “deadwood” accumulated over years—people who have died, permanently shifted, have duplicate registrations or cannot be traced. This should not be viewed as deletion but as cleaning up of the electoral roll. Whoever is available, provides the basic information and signs the form will be included in the draft rolls. Final rolls would include properly mapped electors. Electors who are unmapped or mapped with anomalies will be included in the final rolls on submission of documents. Eligible voters will remain in the electoral roll while non-genuine or obsolete entries will naturally get excluded after due process.
Article Summary: There is widespread concern that genuine voters could be deleted. The guiding principle of the exercise is that no eligible voter should be left out. We have completed mapping around 76% of voters using the previous electoral rolls. It is tentative because similar names may have been wrongly mapped.

Article Title: Trash & Debris Line Mula’s Banks In Pune, Officials Look Away
Article Text: Pune: The river belongs to everyone. Unfortunately, waste dumped on its banks belongs to none. Or at least that is what thousands of residents of a sprawling residential society in the IT hub of Hinjewadi have come to realize, after running behind three different agencies for the past two months, all to get the illegally thrown trash along the river next door cleared.The affected residents of close to 5,000 flats in Blue Ridge Township said it was in April this year that they started seeing huge trucks dumping construction debris as well as domestic waste on private land within 50m of the Mula river.Not new to raising their voice against pollution, the residents thought this dumping would be addressed by authorities, as the waste lies within the flood line, and even moderate swelling of the river in the monsoon would engulf it, leading to pollution and other issues.But despite approaching the Pune Metropolitan Region Development Authority (PMRDA), Maan gram panchayat and Maharashtra Pollution Control Board (MPCB) multiple times, all they found was that the three authorities kept passing the buck.For approximately 17,000 residents of the township, the Mula river is their only drinking water source. The society draws drinking water at a jackwell adjacent to the dumping site.Resident Anirudha Karandikar, an IT professional, said he first noticed the trucks around April 13. "At that time, the frequency was less. But then it started increasing and now, almost eight trucks are seen daily, carrying different types of garbage-construction debris, paint or chemicals, wet waste from restaurants or other places, etc. They come to an area merely 50m from the river and dump the refuse right on the floodplains. "Residents complained that multiple agencies are playing a blame game and eschewing enforcement, without caring for the environmental and public health consequences as the monsoon is heralded. They added that they have registered numerous complaints, from Govt of India's Swachhata-MoHUA app to local grievance redressal cells."When we made a submission on the PMRDA grievance portal, it outright rejected the complaint. We then tried to reach out to Central agencies to route us to the correct state forums. We complained on Central govt's Swachhata-MoHUA app, which sent the complaint back to PMRDA — and it got rejected again," said Karandikar.Resident Sunita Purushottam, who has worked in the field of environment management and sustainability for several years, emphasised the risk posed not only to township residents but also innumerable people downstream. "So much garbage has been dumped here over the months that this could become a chokepoint for the river. How can it be allowed inside the flood line? MPCB says PMRDA gives building permissions, so construction debris management should be the latter's lookout. PMRDA says the area comes under Maan gram panchayat. The gram panchayat says it is the duty of PMRDA to act against violators and that it, as a local authority, cannot issue notices about construction or other activities on private land, which is the revenue department's responsibility. Further, Maan gram panchayat also says MPCB must take punitive action against the private landowner as the dumping site is within the blue/red line. It is a vicious circle," said Purushottam, adding, "There is an urgent need for a systemic overhaul."Moreover, this is not the only dumping site along the Mula river, claimed senior citizen and resident Akash Bokade. "There is also a dumping area at the beginning of a nullah that connects to the river here. If you face towards the flow of the river, there is a new dumping site on the left riverbank. In that entire patch, there are many sites where all kinds of garbage is dumped. To top it all, this trash is burnt at night by people dumping it to make space for more, forcing us to breathe polluted air," he said.Bokade added that people are tired of the constant neglect of authorities. "It is because officials refuse to do their work that we have to run around, visiting each office, complaining about this and that. They are paid for their work with taxpayers' money. We have other things to do and cannot be running around govt offices, reminding officials of their duties," he added.Despite best efforts, citizens faced with lack of accountabilityResidents of Blue Ridge Township in Hinjewadi, under the umbrella of the Blue Ridge Association for Voluntary Engagement (BRAVE), have photographed trucks and registration numbers, mapped GPS coordinates, logged dumping activity dates, filed complaints across six channels, attended MPCB meetings in person, and compiled a chronological evidence trail spanning three months. But govt agencies still need to be nudged repeatedly to even acknowledge the issue, they said. "The Mula river at this point sits across boundaries of nine govt entities. No single agency owns the full problem. Each controls a slice — sewage, floodplain, water, roads, police. The result is diffusion of accountability and procedural paralysis. Even digital shortcut channels — like Centre's Swachh Bharat app — simply route solutions back to the same local authorities that have already declined responsibility," said township resident Anirudha Karandikar.OFFICIAL SPEAKThis whole area is under the jurisdiction of three authorities, namely MIDC, Maan gram panchayat and PMRDA. The spot in question comes under PMRDA. So, if a private party is using land to do any activity, like allowing dumping, they are expected to take permission from PMRDA. We have not given any such permission. We have sent letters to PMRDA to act against the individual. Secondly, since the spot comes under the river's blue/red line, we have asked MPCB to take punitive action. We said in a letter that any environmental damage because of this will not be our responsibility— Madan Shelar | Village development officer, Maan gram panchayatClearance of garbage falls under the jurisdiction of the local governing body. Within Integrated Township Projects (ITPs) such as Blue Ridge Township, solid waste management on-premises is the responsibility of the township management, while waste management outside the township limits falls under the jurisdiction of the gram panchayat. The gram panchayat also has the authority to file an FIR against those responsible for illegal dumping. PMRDA's role is limited to granting development permissions outside the gaothan area and does not extend to solid waste management, which remains the responsibility of the local authority concerned— Abhijit Chaudhari | PMRDA commissioner
Article Summary: Residents of Blue Ridge Township in Hinjewadi, Pune, say they have been seeing trucks dumping construction debris and domestic waste on private land within 50m of the Mula river. The residents thought this dumping would be addressed by authorities, as the waste lies within the flood line, and even moderate swelling of the river in the monsoon would engulf it. But despite approaching the Pune Metropolitan Region Development Authority (PMRDA), Maan gram panchayat and Maharashtra Pollution Control Board (MPCB), all they found was that the three authorities kept passing the buck.

Article Title: Sena (UBT) moves to rebuild Vidarbha base, Uddhav vows Nagpur rally
Article Text: Nagpur: Shiv Sena (UBT) plans to send its senior members to Vidarbha to rebuild a base that has thinned sharply. For this, the UBT is banking on the party's loyalists in the region, a senior functionary of the Uddhav Thackeray-led party said.The functionary, who wanted seniors to declare the plan for rallies, claimed, "The party brass will announce the plan and schedule, but very soon Uddhav Thackeray will come to Nagpur for a massive rally. Senior members will fan out across Vidarbha and later, Aaditya Thackeray will mobilise the youth voters who are yearning for a change," he said.Claiming that UBT has a dedicated vote bank in Vidarbha, the functionary said, "Shiv Sena led by Uddhav is the one that commands loyalty among voters. People connect with him because he was appointed as the party's in charge when Balasaheb was alive," he said.On the 2024 Assembly election results, the functionary claimed, "What happened in 2024 was that voters were given cash incentives with schemes like Ladki Bahin, and people got fooled. Let the 2029 polls come and you will see that the real Shiv Sena will win."The push comes as the party reels from defections. Six of its Lok Sabha MPs recently switched to the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena, and Thackeray is touring the constituencies they represented.Thackeray stopped in Nagpur on Friday for a few minutes to address party workers at the city airport before driving to Yavatmal. "I promise you that I will come to Nagpur specially for you. And when I come, we will hold a proper Shiv Sena style rally," he said then, which led to the party functionaries strategising on future steps. No date has been set for the Nagpur rally.Recalling his earlier rally at Kasturchand Park, Thackeray took a dig at the Mahayuti govt, wondering whether it had left a ground big enough in the city for such a gathering. He struck a defiant note. Thackeray said, "Shiv Sena is here, Shiv Sena will remain. No matter how many generations of opponents come, they cannot finish this party."The six exits came after Shinde hinted at a bigger action to follow, amid talk of "Operation Tiger 3.0" to draw Opposition legislators into his camp.
Article Summary: The push comes as the party reels from defections. Six of its Lok Sabha MPs recently switched to the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena. Thackeray is touring the constituencies they represented. No date has been set for the Nagpur rally.

Article Title: Chennai’s Harshavardhan is India’s 97th GM
Article Text:
Harshavardhan GB became India's 97th chess Grandmaster in Chennai





Nagpur: Chennai’s Harshavardhan GB became India’s 97th chess Grandmaster in his hometown on Sunday.In the second Chola Chess International GM Norm Round Robin Tournament, the 23-year-old Harshavardhan remained unbeaten to secure his third and final norm and also crossed the required 2500 Elo rating to achieve the GM title.The 2493 Elo Harshavardhan won four games, drew five rounds to finish the tournament at the second position with 6.5 points. He performed at a rating of 2620 and gained 15.3 Elo points on his way to cross the requisite 2500 mark.Last year at the Asian Continental Championship in UAE, Harshavardhan achieved his second norm while finishing the event in the ninth position, delivering a performance rating of 2634, and secured his qualification for the FIDE World Cup in Goa. He lost the opening round of the World Cup against Yilmaz Mustafa of Turkey 0.5-1.5.Way back in 2022, Harshavardhan secured his first GM norm during the Asian Junior Championship.In the nine-round GM norm tournament at Chennai, Harshavardhan had a slow start. Of the initial five rounds he scored just three points, and to achieve his final norm, Harshavardhan needed 3.5 points from the remaining four games. He registered three straight victories over Mohan Kushagra, Polish GM Michal Krasenkow, and Bulgarian Alexei Fedorov, respectively, and drew his final round with Mihail Nikitenko to reach his destination.
Article Summary: Chennai's Harshavardhan GB became India’s 97th chess Grandmaster in his hometown on Sunday. In the second Chola Chess International GM Norm Round Robin Tournament, the 23-year-old remained unbeaten to secure his third and final norm.

Article Title: Nandurbar police bust interstate gang of vehicle thieves, recover 32 stolen motorcycles worth over Rs 30 lakh
Article Text:
Stolen bikes recovered by the Nandurbar police

Nashik:





The Nandurbar police have arrested three men and solved 19 motorcycle theft cases spanning Maharashtra, Madhya Pradesh and Gujarat, recovering 32 stolen bikes worth Rs 30.3 lakh, officials said.The breakthrough followed a recent crime review meeting where superintendent of police Ashwini Sanap ordered prompt investigations into pending motorcycle theft cases in the district. Acting on this, the local crime branch (LCB) intensified its probe.Under SP Sanap’s guidance, LCB inspector Hemant Patil and his team analysed past theft patterns, mapped hotspots and tracked activities of recently released vehicle thieves, the police said.On June 26, Patil received a tip-off that two men would arrive at Chikhli Phata in Mhasawad village to sell a stolen black motorcycle. “The LCB team then laid a trap and apprehended two suspects riding a black bike,” the police said.The duo, identified as Balwan alias Ajay Dnyaneshwar Kharde (29) and Himmatsing Sattersing Pawra (29), both from Nandurbar, initially gave evasive answers when asked for vehicle documents, but later confessed to stealing the motorcycle two months ago from near a temple with the help of an accomplice, Rizwan Teli.A case had already been registered at the Dhadgaon police station.Based on their confession, police tracked down and arrested the third accused, Rizwan Teli (29). During interrogation, the three men admitted to stealing multiple motorcycles from Nandurbar and Nashik districts, as well as from neighbouring states like Madhya Pradesh and Gujarat, the police said, adding that the stolen bikes were hidden or distributed among relatives and acquaintances.Acting on their disclosures, the LCB recovered 32 motorcycles from various locations. “Out of the 32 recovered vehicles, 19 have been directly linked to previously registered cases,” the police said. The cases include two in Nandurbar, five in Nashik, six in Madhya Pradesh and six in Gujarat.The police said efforts are on to identify the original owners of the remaining seized motorcycles and verify if more cases are linked to the accused.
Article Summary: The Nandurbar police have arrested three men and solved 19 motorcycle theft cases. The breakthrough followed a recent crime review meeting where superintendent of police Ashwini Sanap ordered prompt investigations. The local crime branch (LCB) intensified its probe.

Article Title: 'Excessive bleeding': Woman dies after attempting home birth by watching YouTube videos in Tamil Nadu; newborn survives
Article Text:
The deceased, identified as Sasikala, was a resident of Thalavaipalayam near Uthukuli. (Representative Image)





COIMBATORE: A young woman lost her life due to excessive bleeding after attempting a home birth by watching YouTube videos near Uthukuli in Tiruppur district.The deceased, identified as Sasikala, was a resident of Thalavaipalayam near Uthukuli.Following the failed delivery attempt at home on June 25, she suffered severe postpartum haemorrhage (excessive bleeding) and was rushed to a government hospital in Perundurai.She was later shifted to a private hospital in Coimbatore.Doctors rescued the baby and kept the newborn under observation. Despite their best efforts, Sasikala succumbed to her condition on Sunday.Preliminary investigations revealed that Sasikala's first child was delivered through a Caesarean section (C-section).Eager to have a natural birth for their second child, she and her husband, along with her mother-in-law, decided to forgo medical supervision. They attempted the delivery at home by following instructional videos on YouTube.During the process, the placenta failed to detach properly, triggering severe and uncontrollable bleeding that ultimately led to her death.Fortunately, the newborn survived the ordeal and is currently under close medical supervision. Meanwhile, the Uthukuli police have registered a case and launched a detailed investigation into the incident.
Article Summary: The deceased, identified as Sasikala, was a resident of Thalavaipalayam near Uthukuli. She suffered severe postpartum haemorrhage (excessive bleeding) and was rushed to a government hospital in Perundurai. Doctors rescued the baby and kept the newborn under observation.

Article Title: Coimbatore policewoman secures gold at national athletics, qualifies for 2026 Asian Games in Japan
Article Text:
J Gowthami clocked 2.04 minutes to cross the finish line first and secured the gold medal





COIMBATORE: J Gowthami, a policewoman with the Coimbatore city police, has clinched a gold medal in an 800-metre running event at the 65th National Inter-State Senior Athletics Championships, being held in Bhubaneswar from June 24 to June 28, and qualified for the upcoming Asian Games in Japan. She clocked 2.04 minutes to cross the finish line first and secured the gold medal.Gowthami (PC 1571), who is serving in the Coimbatore City Armed Reserve, achieved this feat representing the Tamil Nadu state athletics team. This victory has officially qualified Gowthami to represent the Indian athletics contingent at the 20th Asian Games, scheduled to be held in Nagoya, Japan, in Sept 2026.Talking to TOI, Gowthami said: “I am from an agricultural family in Krishnagiri and involved in athletics training from my school days. I secured a gold medal at the Junior National Championship held in 2016. I am working hard to represent our country and make our country proud. Like my parents and husband, higher officials in the department are very encouraging and supportive for me to achieve this level.”Coimbatore city police commissioner N Kannan congratulated Gowthami for bringing pride to the Tamil Nadu police department. The commissioner conveyed his best wishes for her upcoming campaign in Japan, expressing hope that she would continue her winning streak and bring further glory to both the state and the nation.
Article Summary: J Gowthami, a policewoman with the Coimbatore city police, has clinched a gold medal in an 800-metre running event at the 65th National Inter-State Senior Athletics Championships. She clocked 2.04 minutes to cross the finish line first and secured the gold medal.

Article Title: NEET: Salem division to run special trains, announces helplines
Article Text:
Representative image





COIMBATORE: The Salem division of Southern Railway has made special arrangements to facilitate the candidates appearing for the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test (NEET-UG) on Sunday.There are seven towns in the jurisdiction of Salem division where NEET will be conducted. They are Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Coimbatore, Ooty , Namakkal and Karur. Special arrangements have been made across the division, including operation of special trains, to facilitate smooth movement of candidates.Two special trains – one from Chinna Salem to Salem Jn and the other from Tirupattur to Salem Jn – will be operated on Sunday to facilitate candidates appearing for the NEET.Traffic at important level crossing gates will be monitored and regulated to avoid road congestion and ensure smooth movement of candidates to examination centres.Helpdesks will be available at Salem Jn, Erode Jn, Tirupur, Coimbatore Jn, Udagamandalam, Namakkal and Karur Jn railway stations to provide travel related information and assistance to candidates.The helplines: Salem Jn 8778998561, Erode Jn 9840575966, Tiruppur 7904724532, Coimbatore Jn 9384863048, Ooty 7338946729, Namakkal9443516702 and Karur Jn 8056256965.
Article Summary: There are seven towns in the jurisdiction of Salem division where NEET will be conducted. They are Salem, Erode, Tirupur, Coimbatore, Ooty, Namakkal and Karur. Special arrangements have been made across the division.
